data.title: Israel: Escalating strikes against Iran, expanding the scope of operations
data.description: Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated that the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) will further escalate its strikes against Iran, expanding the scope of its operations to cover more targets and areas. Katz made these remarks during a situation assessment meeting with senior IDF officers. He added that Israel had previously warned Iran to cease missile launches against Israeli civilians, but Iran continued its missile launches. Therefore, the IDF will intensify its strikes against Iran. (CBN)

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) has invested an additional $600 million in Polymarket, bringing its total investment to $1.64 billion.
data.description: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) announced the completion of a $600 million cash investment in Polymarket, part of Polymarket's equity financing. ICE also expects to purchase up to $40 million more in Polymarket securities from existing holders. In October 2025, ICE made an initial $1 billion investment in Polymarket. With this additional investment and the anticipated securities purchases, ICE has fulfilled all its obligations under its investment arrangement with Polymarket. ICE stated that the investment in Polymarket is not expected to have a material impact on its financial performance or capital return plans. Specific terms, such as the valuation of the investment, are expected to be disclosed after the Polymarket financing is completed.

data.title: Hong Kong Plans Tax Reform for Asset Management Industry
data.description:
Hong Kong is preparing to implement comprehensive tax reforms for the asset management industry, according to Ming Pao. The proposed legislation, which is set to be reviewed by the Legislative Council, aims to treat profits from investments as performance-based income for tax purposes. This change will extend beyond private equity transactions to include hedge funds, private equity, venture capital, private credit, and even family offices. The new arrangement is expected to further reduce the tax burden for these entities.

Sources indicate that Hong Kong is looking to align itself with financial hubs like Dubai in the United Arab Emirates. The move underscores Hong Kong's commitment to strengthening its position as a global financial center.

data.title: GLM-5.1 Now Accessible to Coding Plan Users
data.description: Zhipu has announced that its GLM-5.1 model is now available to all users subscribed to the Coding Plan. According to NS3.AI, this development aims to enhance user experience by providing advanced capabilities for coding tasks. The release is expected to offer improved performance and efficiency for developers utilizing the platform.

data.title: Machi Big Brother Closes HYPE Position Amid Market Decline
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Hyperbot data revealed that Machi Big Brother has closed his long position in HYPE during the recent market downturn. Meanwhile, his long positions in Ethereum (ETH) and Bitcoin (BTC) are experiencing floating losses of $225,600 and $12,193, respectively.

data.title: Binance Australia's derivatives division was fined $6.9 million for compliance and customer access violations.
data.description: The Federal Court of Australia has ordered Binance Australia's derivatives division, Oztures Trading Pty Ltd, to pay a fine of A$10 million (approximately US$6.9 million). Between July 2022 and April 2023, the entity misclassified over 85% of its local clients as wholesale investors, resulting in 524 retail clients being exposed to high-risk crypto derivatives without statutory consumer protections, causing trading losses of approximately A$8.66 million (approximately US$5.9 million) and fee losses of A$3.9 million (approximately US$2.7 million). Joe Longo, Chairman of the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), stated that Binance failed to establish basic compliance vetting mechanisms and incorrectly approved hundreds of wholesale investor applications. According to the court's statement of fact, Binance admitted to deficiencies in its client onboarding process, allowing applicants to repeatedly take eligibility tests until they passed, and insufficient review of application materials by senior compliance personnel. Binance admitted to six violations, including failing to provide product disclosure statements to retail clients, failing to conduct target market assessments, and failing to maintain a compliant internal dispute resolution system. This fine is in addition to the approximately A$13.1 million (approximately US$9 million) in customer compensation previously overseen by ASIC. The entity's Australian financial services license was cancelled in April 2023.

data.title: Tempo Integrates Safe Multi-Sig Smart Accounts for Institutional Clients
data.description: Tempo has announced the adoption of Safe multi-sig smart accounts tailored for institutional clients. According to NS3.AI, this integration aims to streamline operations by denominating fees in stablecoins and incorporating account abstraction features. These enhancements will enable clients to manage on-chain assets efficiently without the need for gas tokens or external account abstraction layers.

data.title: Canton Network's CC Token Rises 7% as Visa Joins, Bitcoin Falls Over 3%
data.description: Canton Network's CC token experienced a 7% increase following Visa's decision to join the network as a super validator. According to NS3.AI, this development contrasts with Bitcoin's performance, which saw a decline of over 3%, bringing its price down to $66,800. Broader macroeconomic pressures and renewed outflows from spot ETFs continue to impact the wider cryptocurrency market.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase Lowers S&P 500 Target Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: JPMorgan Chase has revised its 2026 year-end target for the S&P 500, reducing it from 7,500 to 7,200. According to NS3.AI, the adjustment is attributed to ongoing tensions in the Middle East and a significant decrease in oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. The bank reported that the number of vessels passing through the strait has dropped to five to six per day, a decline of over 95% from the pre-conflict figure of 138 ships.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Price Predictions for March
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the probability of Bitcoin falling below $65,000 in March has risen to 67% on Polymarket, with a trading volume of approximately $8.24 million. Meanwhile, the likelihood of Ethereum dropping below $1,800 in the same month stands at 21%, with a trading volume of around $1.48 million.

The relevant contract period is from March 1 at 13:00 to April 1 at 11:59, UTC+8.

data.title: An ancient whale transferred 500 BTC to Binance, accumulating a profit of $363 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, 10 minutes ago, a long-time Bitcoin whale (bc1qlf...g6ym) who has held Bitcoin for 13 years transferred 500 BTC to Binance for sale, worth $33.28 million. This whale accumulated 5,000 BTC in November 2013 at a price of $332 and began selling them in November 2024. Currently, it has transferred a total of 4,000 BTC to Binance, at an average price of approximately $91,258, realizing a profit of $363 million. This address currently still holds 1,000 BTC, worth $66.62 million.

data.description: GLM-5.1 is now available to all GLM Coding Plan users (Lite/Pro/Max). (Zhipu)

data.title: Trust Wallet launches TWAK, an AI Agents development kit, bringing AI Agent trading capabilities to 220 million users worldwide.
data.description: According to Forbes, Trust Wallet recently officially released the Trust Wallet Agent Kit (TWAK), enabling its AI Agent to perform on-chain operations across Ethereum-compatible chains and 25 mainstream public chains, including Solana, Bitcoin, TON, and Tron, within user-defined rules. These operations cover token swaps, DCA (Distributed Direct Investment), limit orders, and portfolio management. TWAK offers two modes: one is to equip the AI ​​with an independent wallet, running fully automatically according to preset strategies; the other is to connect to the user's existing wallet, where the AI ​​provides trading suggestions, which the user confirms on a transaction-by-transaction basis. In an interview with Forbes, Trust Wallet's new CEO, Felix Fan, stated, "The agent economy will arrive faster than anyone expects." He also emphasized that TWAK's goal is to "abstract complexity and provide users with a results-oriented, intuitive experience—users only need to express their intentions, and the AI ​​handles the execution details." In the future, Trust Wallet plans to deeply integrate AI functionality into the wallet itself, launching personalized alerts, automated strategies, and trading suggestions. It will also launch an agent strategy marketplace, allowing developers to publish reusable trading bots for users to discover and deploy directly within the app. Felix stated that Trust Wallet's long-term goal is to become the default execution layer for AI agents interacting with the crypto world. He also pointed out that those who participate first will be crypto users with a higher risk tolerance, and Trust Wallet will simultaneously strengthen risk education.

data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Brother Machi closed his HYPE long positions during the market downturn. Additionally, his BTC long positions have a floating loss of $12,193, and his ETH long positions have a floating loss of $225,600.

data.title: Solana Faces Potential Downside as Support Level Tested
data.description: Solana experienced a decline of nearly 5% today, testing support around $84.36. According to NS3.AI, a head-and-shoulders pattern suggests a potential downside of approximately 12% if the neckline is breached. Exchange net outflows decreased from around 2.1 million SOL on March 22 to about 1.3 million SOL by March 26, indicating reduced buying pressure at the support level.

data.title: Large Whale Positions in Oil Futures Observed on Hyperliquid Platform
data.description:
In the past 48 hours, significant whale positions have been identified on the Hyperliquid platform, involving WTIOIL (WTI Crude Oil) and BRENTOIL (Brent Crude Oil) futures. According to BlockBeats, a total of 12 large-scale positions exceeding one million dollars remain open, with five in WTI Crude Oil and seven in Brent Crude Oil. All positions are currently long.

For WTIOIL, the most recent address (0xde8) holds a position valued at $1.83 million, with an average price of $91.4 and a liquidation price of $89.19, approximately 2.4% away from liquidation.

In the case of BRENTOIL, the most recent address (0x8ba) maintains a position worth $1.2 million, with an average price of $103.28 and a liquidation price of $100.58, about 2.6% away from liquidation.

data.title: Tempo and Safe{Wallet} Integrate Programmable Multi-Signature Accounts for Blockchain Payments
data.description:
Tempo has integrated with Safe{Wallet} to introduce programmable multi-signature accounts into blockchain infrastructure aimed at payment scenarios. According to Foresight News, this integration targets financial institutions, payment service providers, and fintech platforms, enabling teams to manage on-chain treasuries with fees priced in stablecoins, with standard TIP-20 token transfer fees below $0.001.

Following the integration, users can initiate transactions through Safe, which are reviewed by designated signatories and executed upon reaching the required multi-signature threshold. Fees are paid directly in USD stablecoins, eliminating the need to hold or manage native Gas tokens. Tempo also supports session keys for delegated signing, offering automated transaction strategies for periodic or rule-based execution, and includes protocol-level account abstraction primitives without relying on external bundlers or paymasters. Tempo is compatible with EVM, allowing existing Safe modules, guards, and tools to be deployed directly, with Safe factory contract addresses consistent with Ethereum.

Currently, Safe{Wallet} secures over $60 billion in assets and manages more than 8 million smart accounts across more than 25 networks.

data.title: Ethereum Falls Below 2000 USDT Amid Daily Decline
data.description: Ethereum has dropped below the 2000 USDT mark, currently trading at 1997.18 USDT. According to Foresight News, this represents a daily decrease of 3.06%.

data.title: Coinbase will launch Katana (KAT) perpetual contracts.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Coinbase will launch Katana (KAT) perpetual contracts today. The KAT-PERP market will open immediately if liquidity conditions are met and trading is supported in the trading area.
